name: HDFC Bank AI Test Suite
scenario: The HDFC Bank AI support system is going to CI. Every test calls the real Anthropic API, costing ₹5,000/run and taking 10 minutes. Build a proper AI test suite: deterministic unit tests with FakeAiService, integration tests with WireMock, and weekly evaluation tests with real AI.

core.description: FakeAiService @Service: Map<String, String> keyword→response. answer(question) scans keywords. Default: 'Service temporarily unavailable.' addResponse(key, value) for test customization.
AiResponseAssertions @Service: containsFact(response, fact) — assertTrue with meaningful message. doesNotHallucinate(response, List<String>) — assertFalse for each banned term.
HdfcAiServiceWithFallback @Service: try { chatClient.call() } catch (Exception) { return cached fallback }. Fallback Map<String, String> for 'balance', 'loan', 'card', 'default'.
EvalDataset @Service: List of 5 EvalCase records (question, List<String> expectedFacts, List<String> bannedTerms). Cases cover: CIBIL, refund, EMI, documents, interest rate.
EvalRunner @Service: run(cases, aiService). Returns EvalResult (total, passed, accuracy %). Passes case when all expectedFacts present AND no bannedTerms present.

@SpringBootTest (5 tests): FakeAi returns scripted response, factual assertion passes, hallucination assertion fails when present, fallback on AI error, eval accuracy > 60% with matching FakeAi. All pass.
README: AI test pyramid — unit (mock/fake), integration (WireMock), evaluation (real AI weekly). Include cost estimate per layer.

bonus.description: WireMock integration test: stub /v1/messages → returns scripted JSON. @DynamicPropertySource redirects spring.ai.anthropic.base-url to WireMock. Verify service returns correct response from stub.

LlmAsJudge @Service: evaluate(question, answer, referenceDoc) → EvaluationResult(score 1-5, feedback, isAcceptable). Score ≥ 3 = acceptable. Test: good response scores ≥ 3, hallucinated response scores < 3.
AiLatencyTracker @Service: record(latencyMs), p50(), p95(), p99(). AiBaselineComparator: alert if p99 regresses > 20%. Tests: percentiles correct, regression detected, no-regression passes.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. FakeAiService vs Mock: FakeAiService has real keyword-matching logic (a Fake) — better for testing multiple scenarios than configuring a Mockito mock chain every test. @MockBean ChatClient.Builder: mocking the builder is the easiest way to control ChatClient in @SpringBootTest — it prevents Spring AI from trying to contact the real API. Factual assertions: use assertTrue(response.contains(fact)) not assertEquals(exactText, response) — AI wording varies, facts don't. WireMock base-url override: spring.ai.anthropic.base-url can be overridden via @DynamicPropertySource to redirect API calls to WireMock. Evaluation accuracy: don't aim for 100% — AI is probabilistic. 80%+ accuracy on your evaluation dataset is a good target.
